description: Land shaped by patient hands over generations, where the earth gives back what has been carefully sown and tended.
biomes: Rows of fruit trees heavy with harvest, their branches low and bending. The air is sweet and bees drift lazily between the blossoms.
Neat lines of grapevines climbing wooden stakes across sun-warmed hillsides. The soil is stony and the fruit is dark and fragrant.
Broad stretches of grain rippling in the wind like a golden sea. The furrows are straight and the work is never finished.
Small, carefully tended plots bursting with rosemary, thyme, and lavender. The scents mingle in the warm air and the bees are fat.
Stepped hillsides carved into flat growing beds, each one irrigated from the one above. The green is vivid against the brown earth.
Fenced grassland where livestock graze under open sky. The ground is clipped short and the fences stretch to the horizon.
Vast ordered rows of a single crop stretching in every direction. The scale is impressive but the sameness makes the land feel hollow.
Small plots packed with vegetables and herbs close to a settlement. The soil is dark from compost and the harvest is always close.
